name: Northwest Territories (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the territory's lakes and many bodies of water
the snow and ice of the Arctic
the polar bear's head on the shield

description: A vertical triband of blue, white, and blue (1:2:1 widths) with the territorial shield centred on the white panel.
history: Adopted by the Council of the Northwest Territories on 1 January 1969 following a flag design competition won by Robert Bessant of Margaret, Manitoba. The design uses the territorial colours and centres the shield from the territorial arms granted in 1956.

features.symbolism: the blue panels represent the lakes and waters of the territory; the white centre represents the snow and ice of the Canadian Arctic

features.role: shield from the arms of the Northwest Territories, granted in 1956
features.symbolism: the territorial shield: a wavy white-and-blue chief representing the Northwest Passage; below, a green pile (representing the boreal forest) on a red ground (the tundra), with two gold rectangles (mineral wealth) and a polar bear's head.
